channel.title: Morning Briefing for George
channel.description: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.
channel.itunes:author: George Cherian
channel.itunes:summary: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, May 28
item.description: In world business, the macro picture is being driven by the U.S.-Iran conflict. Fresh American strikes prompted Iran to target a U.S. airbase, and oil jumped two and a half percent this morning on…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, May 27
item.description: In world business, the dominant story is oil. U.S. crude dropped roughly three percent after Iranian state media released details of a draft peace deal with the U.S. that would reopen the Strait of…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 26
item.description: In world business, oil prices dropped below one hundred dollars a barrel Monday — Brent crude fell roughly six percent to around ninety-seven dollars — on signals that the US and Iran may be nearing…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 25
item.description: In world business, the dominant story is oil. Reports over the weekend suggest the US is close to a deal that would end the conflict with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump confirmed the…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 22
item.description: In world business, Lenovo posted record earnings with AI revenue nearly doubling, sending shares up more than fifteen percent. It's a meaningful data point for enterprise hardware demand — AI-driven…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, May 21
item.description: In world business, the UK has finalized a three-point-seven-billion-pound trade deal with six Gulf states, removing an estimated five hundred eighty million pounds in tariffs on British exports…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, May 20
item.description: In world business, UK inflation came in at two point eight percent for April — lower than the three percent economists expected and down from three point three in March. The relief is likely…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 19
item.description: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 18
item.description: In world business, two stories are moving markets this morning. Oil prices swung sharply after President Trump said he called off planned strikes on Iran, though the Strait of Hormuz remains…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 15
item.description: In world business, oil prices moved higher this morning after reports that China has agreed to purchase American crude following talks between the two governments. That's a meaningful signal for…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, May 14
item.description: In world business, Kevin Warsh was confirmed by the Senate Wednesday as the next Federal Reserve chair, replacing Jerome Powell. The fifty-four to forty-five vote was the most divisive confirmation…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, May 13
item.description: In world business, Tencent reported first-quarter twenty twenty-six earnings showing strength in gaming and AI demand, though overall revenue came in below expectations. That combination — AI and…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 12
item.description: In world business, oil is the dominant story. Prices extended gains this morning after President Trump described the ceasefire with Iran as on "life support," raising concerns about a prolonged…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 11
item.description: In world business, two stories are driving the macro picture this morning. First, oil. Brent crude has crossed one hundred and three dollars a barrel after the Trump administration rejected Iran's…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 8
item.description: In world business, three stories are shaping the macro picture. Oil prices moved higher after a military exchange between US and Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz — President Trump says the…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, May 7
item.description: In world business, the conflict with Iran is reshaping energy markets in real time. Shell posted a nearly twenty-five percent jump in quarterly profits, beating estimates as the Iran war drove…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, May 6
item.description: In world business, the big macro signal overnight is oil. Brent crude dropped to around one hundred one dollars a barrel after President Trump announced a pause in U.S. naval escort operations in…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, May 5
item.description: In world business, Australia's central bank has raised rates again and is warning that inflation will stay elevated longer than expected, citing a pickup in price pressures through the second half…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, May 4
item.description: In world business, oil prices are moving higher after a tanker was struck by projectiles north of Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates. The UK Maritime Trade Operations agency confirmed the incident…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, May 1
item.description: In world business, oil prices dipped after reports that Iran sent a response to a draft peace agreement — a signal that diplomatic channels are still open, which tends to ease supply-risk premiums…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, April 30
item.description: In world business, Apple posted seventeen percent revenue growth in its second quarter, beating estimates even as iPhone unit sales came in below expectations. The real driver was services — a…
